answers.question: Which crates and files will this change affect?
answers.answer: crate:sdd — crates/sdd/src/generate/ (extend existing), projects/score/cli/src/commands.rs (new GenCommands variants)
answers.rationale: Issue scopes explicitly to sdd crate and score CLI entry points

answers.question: Should we use a universal Graph<N,E> or per-diagram Content types?
answers.answer: Per-diagram Content types (D3). Common envelope = {id, title, content, metadata}. Each diagram has its own Content shape. Not a universal Graph<N,E>.
answers.rationale: D3 from issue: requirement diagram has 3 collections, flowchart has nodes/edges, sequence has actors/messages. Per-type is cleaner.

answers.question: What format for CODEGEN block markers?
answers.answer: // CODEGEN-BEGIN and // CODEGEN-END. Codegen updates content inside, wrapper preserved. Multiple blocks per file allowed (one per spec section). SPEC-MANAGED comment above each block.
answers.rationale: D4 from issue: no merge conflicts, hand-written code outside preserved.

answers.question: Which target languages for MVP?
answers.answer: Rust only (D2). Abstract type system designed for multi-language but generators target Rust first. Python/TS deferred.
answers.rationale: D2 from issue: focus on Rust codebase first.

answers.question: What CLI entry points?
answers.answer: score gen diff / apply / render / validate. All 4 integrated into check-alignment. Diff shows drift, apply writes, render regenerates Mermaid body from frontmatter, validate checks frontmatter schema.

answers.question: Should we replace existing XState schemas?
answers.answer: Yes (D8). Existing *_plus/schema.rs are unused XState complexity. Replace with new Graph-based schemas that match codegen targets directly.
answers.rationale: D8 from issue: clean up unused complexity.

answers.question: How does config layering work?
answers.answer: 3 levels: global (.score/config.toml [codegen.*]) -> per-spec (x-rust: extensions in section frontmatter) -> per-file (CODEGEN markers scope generated region).
